market_data_input.key_trends: Secteur leader: information-technology (+26.7% YTD) | Région leader: south-korea (+111.7% YTD) | 5 secteur(s) en refroidissement (3M ≤ -2.0%) | 52W leader: information-technology (+58.4% sur 12 mois) | 6M leader: information-technology (+27.1% sur 6 mois)
market_data_input.risks: Secteurs en difficulté: healthcare, financials | Régions en difficulté: india
market_data_input.sector_momentum_summary: industrials(neutral,b=0.96,d=-1.09%), consumer-discretionary(neutral,b=1.21,d=-1.83%), healthcare(neutral,b=0.53,d=-1.34%), communication-services(neutral,b=1.2,d=1.06%), real-estate(neutral,b=0.43,d=-0.8%), utilities(favored,b=0.37,d=-0.45%), information-technology(favored,b=1.52,d=0.96%), materials(neutral,b=0.8,d=-1.46%), energy(favored,b=-0.15,d=2.36%), financials(avoided,b=0.91,d=-0.29%), consumer-staples(neutral,b=0.24,d=-1.17%)

ai_response.regime_rationale: Brent near $97 with 5-day avg climbing toward $100, CPI at 2.4% but breakeven inflation at 2.52% signaling market expects higher inflation ahead, while Fed is on hold (0 delta 6m) and VIX at 22 reflects elevated uncertainty. Gold at $4458 with silver at $74 confirms inflation hedging demand; IG spreads at 135bps and HYG declining suggest credit stress building — this is early-stage stagflation with energy shock risk.

adjustments.action: Increase energy/oil cap on Brent surge toward $100
adjustments.rationale: Brent at $97 with rising 5-day avg and XLE +2.6% daily confirms energy momentum; stagflation regime historically favors energy producers.

adjustments.action: Reduce AI/semi exposure on stagflation risk
adjustments.rationale: Despite IT leading YTD (+26.7%), QQQ daily change near flat (+0.1%) while energy surges — rotation signal; high-beta tech underperforms in stagflation as real rates rise and margins compress.
Gold at $4458 and silver at $74 despite 16% drawdown from ATH signals massive structural demand; in stagflation gold is the premier real asset hedge.
Consumer staples and utilities provide inflation pass-through and lower beta; financials avoided per RADAR confirms value rotation should be selective toward dividend payers.
Stagflation + geopolitical energy risk + dollar weakening (EUR/USD 1.16) = gold is the primary portfolio insurance.

adjustments.action: Prefer TIPS and shorten duration, avoid HY
adjustments.rationale: Breakeven at 2.52% with Brent near $100 means inflation surprise risk is real; TIPS protect purchasing power while short duration limits rate risk if Fed is forced to hike.

adjustments.action: Increase bond floor for Stable profile
adjustments.rationale: Stable at 37.6% bonds is adequate but should increase slightly given credit deterioration (HYG -0.3%) and stagflation defensiveness.

adjustments.action: Raise tactical cash for dip-buying optionality
adjustments.rationale: VIX at 22 with energy shock building means a vol spike to 30+ is plausible; cash provides optionality to buy the dip if oil triggers a broader selloff.

bond_strategy.regime_summary: Stagflation playbook: TIPS for inflation protection, treasury short-end for safety, avoid HY and securitized as credit stress builds with IG at 135bps and HYG declining.

cash_allocation.rationale: VIX 22 with energy shock risk warrants dry powder; not max cash because existing T-bill positions (CLTL, VGSH) already serve as productive cash proxies earning ~4%.

crypto_allocation.rationale: Crypto is a risk asset that correlates with liquidity; stagflation = tighter liquidity conditions, reduce exposure but maintain small BTC allocation as digital gold hedge in Agressif.

warnings: OIL SHOCK ESCALATION: Brent 5-day avg accelerating from $94 to $97 — if it breaks $100, expect CPI to re-accelerate within 2-3 months, forcing Fed to pivot hawkish and triggering a risk-off cascade.
CREDIT DETERIORATION: IG at 135bps with HYG declining daily — monitor for HY spreads blowing past 400bps which would signal regime shift from stagflation to risk_off.
DOLLAR WEAKNESS TRAP: EUR/USD at 1.16 implies weak dollar which normally supports risk assets, but combined with oil surge it amplifies imported inflation for Europe — watch for ECB hawkish surprise.

GOLD DRAWDOWN ANOMALY: Gold 16% off ATH despite perfect macro backdrop (inflation + weak dollar + geopolitical risk) — suggests forced liquidation or margin calls elsewhere; if gold breaks below $4200, systemic stress is worse than visible.
FINANCIALS SECTOR STRESS: RADAR flags financials as avoided — combined with credit spread widening, monitor regional bank exposure and CRE loan books.

FED POLICY ERROR RISK: Fed on hold at 3.62% with oil surging — if they stay dovish too long, inflation expectations could de-anchor (breakevens already at 2.52%); if they hike, they crash growth. Classic stagflation trap.

market_interpretation.geopolitical_risk: Brent at $97 with sharp daily energy moves (+2.6% XLE) suggests active supply disruption or geopolitical premium — likely Middle East or OPEC+ supply constraint driving an energy shock scenario.

adjustments_output.cash_tactical_rationale: VIX 22 with energy shock risk warrants dry powder; not max cash because existing T-bill positions (CLTL, VGSH) already serve as productive cash proxies earning ~4%.
